description: Badges are small inline labels used for counts, statuses, and categorical indicators. They scale relative to their parent element's font size.

always: Always apply the base `badge` class alongside any color modifier class.
Use <span> for inline badges inside text or other elements.
Empty badges (no text content) are automatically hidden via CSS — this is intentional.
never: Do not use badges as standalone block-level elements — they are inline by design.
Do not apply more than one color modifier class to a single badge.
Do not use badges to convey critical information alone — always pair with visible text for accessibility.

Default.description: Square-cornered filled badge. Use for counts, numeric indicators, and status labels.

Pill.description: Fully rounded badge. Use for tags, compact status chips, or when a softer visual treatment is preferred.

primary.usage: Primary actions, new items, informational status.

InsideButton.description: Badge inside a button to show a count or notification indicator.

InsideHeading.description: Badge inline with a heading to label a section or category.
InsideHeading.example: Outline Icons Icons

InsideNavLink.description: Badge inside a navigation link to show unread or active count.
